com.mentor.chs.plugin.constraint

Interface IXModuleAssignationResult<XMC extends IXModuleCode>

  • All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXFunctionalModuleAssignationResult, IXProductionModuleAssignationResult

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXModuleAssignationResult<XMC extends IXModuleCode>
    Module Assignation Result - holds the return set for module assignation.

    This interface describes the common mechanism for returning the required assignation results for all module assignation constraints.

    See Also:
    IXModuleAssignationObjectAlgorithmConstraint, IXModuleAssignationTechnicalConstraint, IXModuleAssignationConstraint

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Deprecated Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addMessage(java.lang.String message)
      Use this method to report any issues with assignation.
      void addModuleCodeToCombinationForTechnicalModule(java.lang.String techModCode, java.util.Set<XMC> variationScope, java.util.Set<XMC> combination)
      Allows a user to create a new technical module specifying the scope of variation and the combination of module codes in which the that the technical code represents.
      void addModuleCodeToCombinationForTechnicalModule(java.lang.String techModCode, XMC code)

      Allows the user to add a new non-technical module code to be created and assigned to a particular IXModuledObject

      Use this methods to get the list of messages that are about to be displayed as a result of assignation.

      Should non-technical module codes be removed from wires during the assignation process

      By default, implementations of IXModuleAssignationResult populate an object's "Generated" module codes.

      By default, wire objects will retain their non-technical module codes, even after assignation, as wire objects will typically already have module codes assigned.

      Should the objects be assigned?

    • Method Detail

      • shouldObjectsBeAssigned

        boolean shouldObjectsBeAssigned()
        Should the objects be assigned?
        Returns:
        true if the returned codes should be applied.

      • setObjectsToBeAssigned

        void setObjectsToBeAssigned(boolean b)
        Allows the user to specify that the object(s) being processed shouldn't have module codes assigned via "this" constraint. This is useful when multiple constraints are applicable to any given object(s). Setting this value to false would allow subsequent constraints to process the object(s). Setting to true would imply that this constraint should attempt to set module codes. Where no module codes are ultimately found for the object(s), they would not be considered by subsequent constraints.
        Parameters:
        b - true if the objects should be assigned

      • getModuleCodeMap

        java.util.Map<IXModuledObject,java.util.Set<XMC>> getModuleCodeMap()
        Returns:
        Map holding IXModuleObject .v. set of proposed existing IXModuleCodes to be assigned

      • getNewTechnicalModuleCodeMap

        java.util.Map<IXModuledObject,java.util.Set<java.lang.String>> getNewTechnicalModuleCodeMap()
        Returns:
        Map holding IXModuleObject .v. set of proposed new technical module codes to be created and assigned

      • getNewNonTechnicalModuleCodeMap

        java.util.Map<IXModuledObject,java.util.Set<java.lang.String>> getNewNonTechnicalModuleCodeMap()
        Returns:
        Map holding IXModuleObject .v. set of proposed new non-technical module codes to be created and assigned

      • getTechModuleCombinationMap

        @Deprecated
java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.util.Set<XMC>> getTechModuleCombinationMap()
        Deprecated. you should now use @link #getTechModuleMap() getTechModuleMap.
        This method is no longer supported and will return a map of technical code to combination without any contextual variation information.

        This interface is not rich enough to provide distinct technical modules where the codes in the variation occurred is the same, but the scope of variation is different. For instance at a connector1 there may be three codes F1, F2, F3 and at connector2 there may be three codes F1, F2, Fx; knowing a technical code represents the combination is F1, F2 is not enough you also need to know the variation scope (i.e. For connector1 it woul be F1, F2, FM3; for connector2 it woudl be F1, F2, Fx).

        Returns:
        Map holding technical module code name .v. set of module codes that the technical code is appliable too.

      • getTechModuleMap

        java.util.Map<java.lang.String,IXTechnicalCombination<XMC>> getTechModuleMap()
        Returns:
        Map holding new technical modules, the key holds the name and the value holds the technical module definition.

      • addModuleCodeToObject

        void addModuleCodeToObject(IXModuledObject object,
                           XMC code)
        Allows the user to add an existing IXModuleCode to be assigned to a particular IXModuledObject
        Parameters:
        object - the object to be assigned
        code - the existing IXModuleCode to be added

      • addNewNonTechnicalModuleCodeToObject

        void addNewNonTechnicalModuleCodeToObject(IXModuledObject object,
                                          java.lang.String code)
        Allows the user to add a new non-technical module code to be created and assigned to a particular IXModuledObject
        Parameters:
        object - the object to be assigned
        code - the String value of the non-technical code to be created and assigned.

      • addNewTechnicalModuleCodeToObject

        void addNewTechnicalModuleCodeToObject(IXModuledObject object,
                                       java.lang.String code)
        Allows the user to add a new technical module code to be created and assigned to a particular IXModuledObject
        Parameters:
        object - the object to be assigned
        code - the String value of the technical code to be created and assigned.

      • addModuleCodeToCombinationForTechnicalModule

        @Deprecated
void addModuleCodeToCombinationForTechnicalModule(java.lang.String techModCode,
                                                               XMC code)
        Deprecated. please use addModuleCodeToCombinationForTechnicalModule(String, Set, Set)
        No longer supported, please use addModuleCodeToCombinationForTechnicalModule(String, Set, Set). See getTechModuleCombinationMap() for why this interface is not rich enough.

        Allows the user to add applicable module codes to a newly generated technical module (without specifying any contextual variation information and thus the contextual variation is assumed to be the combination module codes).

        For example:

        A new technical module code TM1 may be applicable to functional module codes F1,F2

        This would be achieved by calling addModuleCodeToCombinationForTechnicalModule() passing in the String value for the technical module code and the existing module code objects to be assigned. At the end of the assignation action, the system will create and populate the module code "combinations" against the technical module code specified.

        Parameters:
        techModCode - the technical module code to be added to
        code - the existing module code to be added to the list of module codes applicable to this new technical module.

      • addModuleCodeToCombinationForTechnicalModule

        void addModuleCodeToCombinationForTechnicalModule(java.lang.String techModCode,
                                                  java.util.Set<XMC> variationScope,
                                                  java.util.Set<XMC> combination)
        Allows a user to create a new technical module specifying the scope of variation and the combination of module codes in which the that the technical code represents.

        For example:

        A tube exists where three wires with different module codes (FM1, FM2, FM3) running through it. Here the scope of the variation is FM1, FM2, FM3, and there will be up to 7 technical codes that represent the set of combinations of the wires that could go through it (depending on code relationships). To create a new technical module code TM1 where the combination FM1,FM2 go through it you would call this method with "TM1", [FM1, FM2, FM3], [FM1, FM2].

        At the end of the assignation action, the system will create and populate the module code "combinations" against the technical module code specified.

        Parameters:
        techModCode - the technical module code name
        variationScope - the scope of the variation for this technical module code
        combination - the technical module's combination of content

      • getModuledObjectsWhoseTechCodesNeedRemoving

        java.util.Set<IXModuledObject> getModuledObjectsWhoseTechCodesNeedRemoving()
        Allows the user to get all the IXModuledObjects whose technical module codes need to be removed.

        Is should be noted that this will be applied before any new codes are added.

        Returns:
        the set of IXModuledObject

      • removeTechnicalModuleCodesFromObject

        void removeTechnicalModuleCodesFromObject(IXModuledObject object)
        Allows the user to remove all technical module codes from a particular IXModuledObject
        Parameters:
        object - the object whose codes should be cleared

      • addMessage

        void addMessage(java.lang.String message)
        Use this method to report any issues with assignation.

        The message will appear in the processing tab of the output window, or the application log file if no output window is available for the current application.

        Parameters:
        message - - the message to display.

      • getMessages

        java.util.List<java.lang.String> getMessages()
        Use this methods to get the list of messages that are about to be displayed as a result of assignation.
        Returns:
        the list of messages.

      • getModuledDesign

        IXModuledDesign getModuledDesign()
        Get the IXModuledDesign that this result object is for. Using this method you can obtain the codes that can be applied to the design. For example:

        IXModuledDesign.getAvailableFunctionalModuleCodes() and IXModuledDesign.getAvailableProductionModuleCodes()

        You can also get the available combinations for technical module codes:

        IXModuledDesign.getFunctionalModuleCodeCombinations() and IXModuledDesign.getProductionModuleCodeCombinations()
        Returns:
        the IXModuledDesign that this result object is for.

      • populateUserModuleCodes

        void populateUserModuleCodes()
        By default, implementations of IXModuleAssignationResult populate an object's "Generated" module codes. Calling this method allows the plugin to instruct the assignation process to write to an object's "User" module codes. Note that this affects all object types. This method has been added at 2012.1. Plugins created prior to 2012.1 will remain unchanged.

      • isPopulatingUserCodes

        boolean isPopulatingUserCodes()
        Does this assignation result write to "User" module codes?
        Returns:
        true if writing to "User" module codes, otherwise false (default) to indicate writing to "Generated" module codes

      • removeNonTechnicalCodesFromWires

        void removeNonTechnicalCodesFromWires()
        By default, wire objects will retain their non-technical module codes, even after assignation, as wire objects will typically already have module codes assigned. Calling this method allows the plugin to instruct the assignation process to remove all non-technical codes from wire objects during assignation, replacing the codes with any codes specified by the plugin. Note that this method only affects wires. It has no affect on other object types. This method has been added at 2012.1. Plugins created prior to 2012.1 will remain unchanged.

      • isClearingNonTechnicalCodesFromWires

        boolean isClearingNonTechnicalCodesFromWires()
        Should non-technical module codes be removed from wires during the assignation process
        Returns:
        true if non-technical codes should be removed from wire objects.
